src: γίνεται δὲ τό ποτε τίμιον ἄτιμον
lit: Then the once-precious becomes dishonored
sm: Then what was once precious becomes dishonored

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the following genitive absolute in line 252 supplies the temporal condition.
syn.svo: Subject: τό ποτε τίμιον. Verb: γίνεται. Predicate adjective: ἄτιμον.
syn.rhet: The sentence launches the key reversal that governs the next few lines.

tok: aor pass ptcp gen sg n
lit: when the naturally precious has been revealed.
sm: when what is precious by nature has been revealed.

syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; the genitive absolute supplies the temporal circumstance.
syn.svo: No finite subject or verb in the main clause of this line. Participial construction: τοῦ φύσει τιμίου φανερωθέντος.
syn.ptc: φανερωθέντος is the participial core of the genitive absolute.
syn.rhet: The line explains why the formerly precious becomes dishonored: revelation resets value.

lit: For each [thing] has its own time,
sm: For each thing has its own time,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a comma.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Dative of reference: ἑκάστῳ. Predicate adjective: ἴδιος. Subject noun: καιρός, with an implied copula.
syn.rhet: Gives the governing principle for the distinction between type, material, and truth.

lit: An own time of the type,
sm: The type has its own time,

syn.mc: Nominal clause in apposition to the temporal principle of line 253.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: χρόνος. Predicate adjective: ἴδιος. Genitive modifier: τοῦ τύπου.
syn.rhet: First member of a threefold temporal parallelism.

lit: An own time of the material,
sm: the material has its own time,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: χρόνος. Predicate adjective: ἴδιος. Genitive modifier: τῆς ὕλης.
syn.rhet: Second member of the temporal triad.

lit: An own time of the truth.
sm: the truth has its own time.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: χρόνος. Predicate adjective: ἴδιος. Genitive modifier: τῆς ἀληθείας.
syn.rhet: Completes the threefold sequence type, material, truth.

lit: You make the type,
sm: You make the type,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a comma.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied σύ. Verb: ποιεῖς. Object: τὸν τύπον.
syn.rhet: The direct address shifts from principle to the act of typological construction.

lit: This one you desire
sm: you desire this one

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied σύ. Verb: ποθεῖς. Object: τοῦτον.
syn.rhet: The object is singled out by demonstrative force.

src: ὅτι τοῦ μέλλοντος ἐν αὐτῷ τὴν εἰκόνα βλέπεις.
tok: pres act ptcp gen sg n
lit: because you see the image of the future in it.
sm: because you see the image of the future in it.

syn.mc: Subordinate explanatory clause dependent on line 258.
syn.sub: ὅτι introduces the reason for desiring the type.
syn.svo: Subject: implied σύ. Verb: βλέπεις. Object: τὴν εἰκόνα. Genitive phrase: τοῦ μέλλοντος. Locative phrase: ἐν αὐτῷ.
syn.rhet: The desire for the type is justified by its anticipatory visibility.

lit: You bring forward the material to the type,
sm: you bring the material forward to the type,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a comma.

syn.mc: Main second-person clause continuing the typological address.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied σύ. Verb: προκομίζεις. Object: τὴν ὕλην. Dative: τῷ τύπῳ.
syn.rhet: The material is actively set in relation to the type, not left inert.

lit: This one you desire
sm: you desire this one

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied σύ. Verb: ποθεῖς. Object: ταύτην.
syn.rhet: The demonstrative is now feminine, matching ὕλην from the omitted line 262 context.

src: διὰ τὸ μέλλον ἐν αὐτῇ ἀνίστασθαι.
tok: pres act ptcp acc sg n
to rise; to come into being
lit: because the future is to rise in it.
sm: because the future is to arise in it.

syn.mc: Subordinate causal/infinitival clause dependent on line 263.
syn.sub: διὰ τὸ + infinitive expresses cause or purpose.
syn.svo: Infinitive phrase: ἀνίστασθαι. Temporal subject: τὸ μέλλον. Locative phrase: ἐν αὐτῇ.
syn.rhet: The type is desired because it contains the future as latent reality.

lit: You complete the work,
sm: you complete the work,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a comma.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied σύ. Verb: ἀπαρτίζεις. Object: τὸ ἔργον.
syn.rhet: The act of completion follows naturally from desire and projection.

lit: This alone you desire,
sm: you desire only this,

syn.mc: Main clause in a chain of direct address.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied σύ. Verb: ποθεῖς. Object: τοῦτο. Adverb: μόνον.
syn.rhet: Restrictive repetition sharpens the speaker's focus.

lit: This alone you love,
sm: you love only this,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied σύ. Verb: φιλεῖς. Object: τοῦτο. Adverb: μόνον.
syn.rhet: The paired verbs ποθεῖς / φιλεῖς intensify the exclusive attachment to the type.

src: ἐν αὐτῷ μόνῳ τὸν τύπον καὶ τὴν ὕλην καὶ τὴν ἀλήθειαν βλέπων.
tok: pres act ptcp nom sg m
lit: Seeing the type and the material and the truth in it alone.
sm: seeing the type, the material, and the truth in it alone.

syn.mc: Participial clause dependent on the implied second-person subject.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; βλέπων explains the grounds for the desire and love of lines 266-267.
syn.svo: Participle: βλέπων. Objects: τὸν τύπον, τὴν ὕλην, τὴν ἀλήθειαν. Locative phrase: ἐν αὐτῷ μόνῳ.
syn.ptc: βλέπων is circumstantial, summing up the typological perception.
syn.rhet: A triadic list completes the phenomenology of typology: type, matter, truth.

ana: Ὡς γοῦν ἐν τοῖς φθαρτοῖς παραδείγμασιν,
lit: As, then, in the corruptible examples,
sm: As, then, in the corruptible examples,
emd: Leading OCR quotation mark removed in analysis.

syn.mc: Comparative clause introducing the formal analogy.
syn.sub: No finite verb; the comparison is completed by line 271 and expanded by 272-273.
syn.svo: Prepositional phrase: ἐν τοῖς φθαρτοῖς παραδείγμασιν.
syn.rhet: Begins the explicit analogy from earthly models to heavenly ones.

src: οὕτως δὴ καὶ ἐν τοῖς ἀφθάρτοις"
ana: οὕτως δὴ καὶ ἐν τοῖς ἀφθάρτοις,
lit: thus indeed also in the incorruptible things,
sm: so indeed also in the incorruptible things,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a comma.

syn.sub: Still part of the same comparison; the corresponding ὡς-phrase follows in lines 272-273.
syn.svo: Prepositional phrase: ἐν τοῖς ἀφθάρτοις.
syn.rhet: The earthly/heavenly contrast is stated in balanced antithesis.

lit: as in the earthly things,
sm: as in the earthly things,

syn.sub: No finite verb; line 273 supplies the correlative half.
syn.svo: Prepositional phrase only.
syn.rhet: Repeats the comparative pattern to seal the analogy.

src: οὕτω δὴ καὶ ἐν τοῖς ἐπουρανίοις.
lit: so indeed also in the heavenly things.
sm: so indeed also in the heavenly things.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause beyond the correlative comparison.
syn.svo: Prepositional phrase only.
syn.rhet: Heavenly reality corresponds to earthly models.

src: καὶ γὰρ ἡ τοῦ κυρίου σωτηρία καὶ ἀλήθεια ἐν τῷ λαῷ προετυπώθη,
lit: For also the Lord's salvation and truth were prefigured in the people,
sm: For the Lord's salvation and truth were prefigured in the people,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; γάρ explains the preceding analogy by grounding it in scriptural typology.
syn.svo: Subject: ἡ τοῦ κυρίου σωτηρία καὶ ἀλήθεια. Verb: προετυπώθη. Locative phrase: ἐν τῷ λαῷ.
syn.rhet: Moves from analogy to explicit typological claim.

src: καὶ τὰ τοῦ εὐαγγελίου δόγματα ὑπὸ τοῦ νόμου προεκηρύχθη.
lit: And the teachings of the gospel were proclaimed beforehand by the law.
sm: And the teachings of the gospel were proclaimed beforehand by the law.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: τὰ τοῦ εὐαγγελίου δόγματα. Verb: προεκηρύχθη. Agent phrase: ὑπὸ τοῦ νόμου.
syn.rhet: Law and gospel are aligned as prefiguration and proclamation.

src: ἐγένετο οὖν ὁ λαὸς τύπος προκεντήματος
lit: Therefore the people became a type of pattern,
sm: Therefore the people became a patterned type,

syn.mc: Main clause summarizing the typological logic.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ λαός. Verb: ἐγένετο. Predicate nominative: τύπος. Genitive modifier: προκεντήματος.
syn.rhet: The people themselves are recast as a typological pattern.

lit: and the law [became] writing of a parable,
sm: and the law became the writing of a parable,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a comma.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; ἐγένετο is understood from line 276.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ νόμος. Implied verb: ἐγένετο. Predicate nominative: γραφή. Genitive modifier: παραβολῆς.
syn.rhet: The law is redefined as scriptural parable.

src: τὸ δὲ εὐαγγέλιον διήγημα νόμου καὶ πλήρωμα,
lit: But the gospel [became] a narrative of law and fulfillment,
sm: but the gospel became a narrative of law and fulfillment,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; ἐγένετο remains understood from line 276.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ εὐαγγέλιον. Implied verb: ἐγένετο. Predicate nominatives: διήγημα and πλήρωμα. Genitive modifier: νόμου.
syn.rhet: The gospel is cast as the fulfillment and narrative counterpart to the law.

src: ἡ δὲ ἐκκλησία ἀποδοχεῖον τῆς ἀληθείας.
lit: And the church [became] a receptacle of the truth.
sm: and the church became a receptacle of the truth.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; ἐγένετο is understood from line 276.
syn.svo: Subject: ἡ ἐκκλησία. Implied verb: ἐγένετο. Predicate nominative: ἀποδοχεῖον. Genitive modifier: τῆς ἀληθείας.
syn.rhet: The church is presented as the vessel of truth.

src: ἦν οὖν ὁ τύπος τίμιος πρὸ τῆς ἀληθείας
lit: Therefore the type was precious before the truth
sm: Therefore the type was precious before the truth

syn.mc: Main clause beginning a comparative sentence.
syn.sub: The comparison is completed in line 281 outside the batch.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ τύπος. Verb: ἦν. Predicate adjective: τίμιος. Comparative phrase: πρὸ τῆς ἀληθείας.
syn.rhet: Introduces the valuation contrast between type and truth that the next line completes.
